items.misconception: 14, because you read and calculate left to right (3 + 4 = 7, then 7 x 2).
items.random_wrong: 9, because you add all the numbers and then subtract the operator count.
items.why: Students compute left-to-right by default, ignoring the precedence rules in BIDMAS/BODMAS.

items.correct: 18 — division and multiplication share the same precedence; work left to right (6 x 3).
items.misconception: 2, because in BIDMAS multiplication comes before division (12 / 6).
items.random_wrong: 7, because you do 12 / 2 = 6 and then add 3 with the multiplication symbol meaning 'and'.
items.why: Students treat BIDMAS as a strict order (M before D), missing that multiplication and division have equal priority and are evaluated left to right.

items.q: What is the reciprocal of the mixed number 2 1/3?
items.correct: 3/7 — first convert to the improper fraction 7/3, then flip it.
items.misconception: 1/2 1/3, by flipping each part of the mixed number separately.
items.why: Students don't convert mixed numbers to improper fractions before taking the reciprocal, and confuse 'reciprocal' with 'negative'.
Correct answer: 14. “64” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.
It's 10. “90” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.
The real figure is 22. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
